content.problemStatement:
Chef has an array AAA of length NNN.

He defines the beauty of the array to be the bitwise AND of all the numbers in the array, i.e,

A1 & A2 & ‚Ä¶ & ANA_1\ \&\ A_2\ \&\ \ldots\ \&\ A_NA1‚Äã & A2‚Äã & ‚Ä¶ & AN‚ÄãChef asks you to answer QQQ queries on this array.

In each query,


You are given two integers XXX and KKK.
**At most** KKK times, you can do the following operation:

Choose two integers LLL and RRR (1‚â§L‚â§R‚â§N1 \leq L \leq R \leq N1‚â§L‚â§R‚â§N).
Then, for each index L‚â§i‚â§RL \leq i \leq RL‚â§i‚â§R, replace AiA_iAi‚Äã with Ai‚äïXA_i \oplus XAi‚Äã‚äïX.

Here, ‚äï\oplus‚äï denotes the bitwise XOR operation.


The answer to the query is the **maximum** possible beauty of the array AAA after performing the given operation at most KKK times.
Note that the array doesn't actually change here, i.e, each query should be answered assuming you start from the original array AAA.

**Warning:** This problem's input data is large, using faster methods of input is recommended. This page has recommendations for C++, Java, and Python.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain an integer TTT - the number of testcases.
Each test case consists of multiples lines of input.

The first line of each testcase will contain two space-separated integers NNN and QQQ ‚Äî the number of elements in the array and the number of queries, respectively.
The second line of each testcase will contain NNN space-separated integers A1,A2,‚Ä¶,ANA_1,A_2,\ldots ,A_NA1‚Äã,A2‚Äã,‚Ä¶,AN‚Äã, denoting the array AAA.
The next QQQ lines will describe the queries. The iii-th of these lines contains two space-separated integers KKK and XXX, the parameters for the iii-th query.




Output Format
For each testcase, for each query, output on a new line the maximum possible beauty of the array.
constraints: The sum of NNN over all testcases does not exceed 2‚ãÖ1052\cdot 10^52‚ãÖ105.
The sum of QQQ over all testcases does not exceed 6‚ãÖ1056\cdot 10^56‚ãÖ105.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Query 111: We have X=3X = 3X=3 and K=5K = 5K=5. Consider the following sequence of operations:

First, choose L=R=1L = R = 1L=R=1, and replace A1A_1A1‚Äã with A1‚äï3A_1\oplus 3A1‚Äã‚äï3.
The array becomes [2,2,3,4,5][2, 2, 3, 4, 5][2,2,3,4,5].
Then, choose L=4L = 4L=4 and R=5R = 5R=5, replacing A4A_4A4‚Äã and A5A_5A5‚Äã.
The array becomes [2,2,3,7,6][2, 2, 3, 7, 6][2,2,3,7,6].

The beauty of the array is 222. It can be shown that this is the maximum possible.
Query 222: Now, X=4X = 4X=4 and K=1K = 1K=1.
Chef can perform the operation with L=1L = 1L=1 and R=3R = 3R=3, resulting in the array [5,6,7,4,5][5, 6, 7, 4, 5][5,6,7,4,5].
The beauty of this array is 444, which is the maximum possible.
